name: Athlete with knee injury — no impact loading
description: Recovering from patellar tendinopathy. Wants to maintain fitness but cannot run, jump, or squat deep. Plan must respect this constraint absolutely — any heavy knee-loading is a blocker.
factsBlock:
Athlete facts (MUST RESPECT):
- Knee injury — patellar tendinopathy diagnosed 2026-04-10. No running, jumping, or deep squatting until cleared. Cycling and swimming are fine. Upper-body lifting unrestricted.
- Prefers swim on Tuesdays and Thursdays (lap pool access).
must_have: Zero running sessions across all weeks
No back squat, front squat, jump squat, lunges, or other deep-knee-loading movements
Cycling and/or swimming as the cardio modalities
Upper-body lifting present (chest/back/shoulders/arms) — this is allowed and athlete still wants to train
Tuesday and Thursday biased toward swim sessions per the athlete's facts
must_not_have: Any session involving running (treadmill, outdoor, intervals — all banned)
Squats, lunges, leg press, jumps, plyometrics in any session
Heavy hinge work (deadlift) without an explicit modification note
